annotations.label: Poisson distribution

anchor.snippet: is a discrete probability distribution that expresses the probability of a given number of events occurring in a fixed interval of time

annotations.note: `poissonMeasure r` defines the Poisson distribution on ℕ with rate `r`.

anchor.snippet: the probability of k events in the same interval is

annotations.note: `poissonMeasure_singleton` gives `P{n} = exp(-r) r^n / n!`, the stated PMF.

anchor.snippet: The Poisson distribution is also the limit of a binomial distribution

annotations.label: Overflow floods

anchor.section: Examples of probability for Poisson distributions
anchor.snippet: On a particular river, overflow floods occur once every 100 years on average

annotations.label: World Cup goals

anchor.section: Examples of probability for Poisson distributions
anchor.snippet: the average number of goals in a World Cup soccer match is approximately 2.5

annotations.note: A worked numerical example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Student union arrivals

anchor.section: Examples that violate the Poisson assumptions
anchor.snippet: The number of students who arrive at the student union per minute will likely not follow a Poisson distribution

annotations.label: Earthquake aftershocks

anchor.section: Examples that violate the Poisson assumptions
anchor.snippet: The number of magnitude 5 earthquakes per year in a country may not follow a Poisson distribution

annotations.note: An informal counterexample, not formalized.

annotations.label: Guaranteed event

anchor.section: Examples that violate the Poisson assumptions
anchor.snippet: Examples in which at least one event is guaranteed are not Poisson distributed

annotations.label: Excess zeros

anchor.section: Examples that violate the Poisson assumptions
anchor.snippet: Count distributions in which the number of intervals with zero events is higher than predicted by a Poisson model

anchor.snippet: The expected value of a Poisson random variable is λ

annotations.label: Limiting case of binomial

anchor.section: As a Binomial distribution with infinitesimal time-steps
anchor.snippet: The Poisson distribution can be derived as a limiting case to the binomial distribution as the number of trials goes to infinity

annotations.note: The binomial→Poisson limit as `n→∞` with `np→r` is proved by the Poisson limit theorem.

annotations.label: Binomial approximation criteria

anchor.section: As a Binomial distribution with infinitesimal time-steps
anchor.snippet: The Poisson distribution is a good approximation of the binomial distribution if n is at least 20 and p is smaller than or equal to 0.05

annotations.note: These heuristic numerical approximation criteria are not formalized.

annotations.label: Poisson limit theorem

anchor.snippet: the binomial distribution converges to what is known as the Poisson distribution by the Poisson limit theorem

annotations.note: This is exactly the formalized Poisson limit theorem (law of rare events).

anchor.snippet: The variance of the binomial distribution is 1 − p times that of the Poisson distribution

annotations.note: The binomial/Poisson variance ratio statement is not formalized.

annotations.note: A biology application example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Katz and Miledi membrane potential

anchor.snippet: Katz and Miledi measured the membrane potential with and without the presence of acetylcholine (ACh)

annotations.note: A biology application example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Mutations per replication

anchor.snippet: During each cellular replication event, the number of mutations is roughly Poisson distributed

annotations.note: A biology application example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Poisson noise / shot noise

anchor.snippet: These fluctuations are denoted as Poisson noise or (particularly in electronics) as shot noise

annotations.note: Poisson/shot noise is an applied concept, not formalized.

annotations.label: Causal set spacetime elements

anchor.snippet: In causal set theory the discrete elements of spacetime follow a Poisson distribution in the volume

annotations.note: A physics application example, not formalized.

annotations.label: Coherent state energy measurement

anchor.snippet: for a quantum harmonic oscillator system in a coherent state , the probability of measuring a particular energy level has a Poisson distribution

annotations.note: The coherent-state photon-number Poisson statistics result is not formalized.
